title: Dim an LED with a Potentiometer
description: Adjust LED brightness using a potentiometer and an Arduino.

dialogue.text: Orion here again! Ready to add some interactivity? We'll use a potentiometer as a dimmer for your LED. Make sure the Arduino is unplugged before wiring.

options.text: Sounds good! What do I need?

dialogue.text: Place a potentiometer on your breadboard. Disconnect power, then connect the side pins to 5V and ground and the wiper to analog pin A0. Keep the LED on pin 9 through a 220 Ω resistor to limit current. Use jumper wires to make the connections.

Plug the Arduino back in and open the Arduino IDE. Read the potentiometer with analogRead(A0) and map the 0–1023 value to 0–255 before analogWrite(9, result) to dim the LED. The 'AnalogInOutSerial' example shows the basics.

options.text: Uploaded! The LED changes brightness as I turn the knob.

dialogue.text: Great job! Potentiometers open up lots of control options. Always disconnect power before changing the circuit, and try using the knob to control motors or sensor thresholds next!
